bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueD, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue and gives no evidence yet of willingness to make balanced concessions.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueD offer that is worst for me. To avoid simply repeating my previous exact bid while still protecting my high-weight issues, I concede only on the lowest-weight issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeatedly offered the same all-valueD bid, which is very low utility for me and shows no concession pattern. To avoid repeating my last bid while making only a small early concession, I am conceding further only on the lowest-weight issueD.

I am targeting a high utility offer around 0.92, since it is still very early at t=0.06 and there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueD with no concession, so I will only concede on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high but modestly more flexible utility bid (about 0.866), since it is still very early at t=0.08 and the opponent has shown no concession from their all-valueD offers. Their repeated bids suggest they may strongly prefer valueD across issues, so I continue conceding first on my lower-weight issues while holding firm on the most important issues A and B.

I targeted a still-high utility bid around 0.81 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering all valueD, which is low utility for me. Since time pressure is minimal at t=0.10, I am only conceding on lower-weight issues first while holding firm on issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid of about 0.76 by holding firm on the two most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding on lower-weight issues. Since it is still early at t=0.12, I should not move too far toward the opponent’s repeated all-valueD offer, but their complete lack of concession suggests testing a clearer concession on issueC. There are no opponent messages to weigh, and all prior offers used valid domain values.

The opponent has repeated the all-valueD bid with no concession or messages, so I am trusting the offer history rather than assuming flexibility. At t=0.14 there is still time, but to avoid deadlock and avoid repeating my last bid, I am making a moderate concession on issueB while holding firm on the highest-weight issueA; this targets about 0.679 utility for me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility by holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while conceding the lower-weight issues C and D. The opponent has repeatedly offered the same all-valueD bid with no concession, and since it is still early at t=0.16, I should not over-concede further just to chase acceptance.

At t=0.18 it is still early, and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering the same low-utility bid for me. I am targeting a high utility while avoiding repeating my exact previous bid, keeping my strongest issues issueA and issueB at valueA and conceding mainly on lower-weight issues.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility around 0.76 while showing some concession on my lower-weight issues. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueD with no concession, so I will not concede on my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB yet, but I can move issueC and issueD to valueD to test whether that helps.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.81 because it is still early in the negotiation and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering the same lowest-utility package for me. I will continue holding firm on the most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding mainly on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 because it is early enough that I should not chase the opponent's unchanged all-valueD position. The opponent has made no concessions at all, so I am holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while varying the lower-weight issues to avoid simply repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early-mid negotiation time and the opponent has shown no concession, repeatedly offering the same low-utility all-valueD bid. I will keep firm on the most important issues A and B while conceding on lower-weight issues C and D, and avoid repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.813 while making a small additional concession because we are past the opening phase but not near the deadline. The opponent has held completely firm at all valueD offers with no messages, so I will protect my highest-weight issues A and B while varying concessions on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.76 by holding firm on the two highest-weight issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding issueC and issueD to the opponent’s consistently signaled preference. At t=0.30 there is still time left, but the opponent has not conceded at all from valueD on every issue, so I am making a moderate compromise without dropping the most important issues.

I targeted a utility around 0.79, keeping my high-weight issues A and B at valueA while making concessions on the lower-weight issues C and D. Since it is still relatively early-mid negotiation time and the opponent has held firm on all valueD offers without any concession, I should not move heavily toward their demand yet, but I also avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.76 by holding firm on the two most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding the lower-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to justify giving up high-weight issues, especially since the opponent has shown no concession at all and keeps repeating all valueD offers. No opponent messages were provided, so I am relying only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a utility around 0.81 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A and B while conceding more on lower-weight issues C and D. At t=0.36 there is still time, and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueD, so I should not drop too far yet. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a still-strong utility by holding firm on my highest-weight issueA while conceding issueC and issueD fully and making a moderate concession on issueB. At t=0.38 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s unchanged all-valueD position, which gives me very low utility. The opponent has shown no concession and sent no messages, so I am using a controlled concession rather than matching their demand.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76 by holding firm on the most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding issueC and issueD to valueD. The opponent has repeatedly offered only valueD on every issue with no concession pattern and no messages, so at t=0.40 I should not drop toward their very low-utility bid yet, but I should continue showing flexibility on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.679 by holding firm on my most important issueA while conceding on lower-weight issues and moving issueB one step off my top value. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueD with no concession, and there are no messages to add credible information, so at t=0.42 I will make a moderate concession without dropping near their extreme position.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.60, which is a meaningful mid-game concession while still well above the opponent's repeated all-valueD offer worth only 0.20 to me. The opponent has held completely firm with the same all-D bid and provided no messages, so I will protect my most important issueA while conceding further on issueB and leaving the lower-weight issues at valueD.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.52 by holding firm on the highest-weight issueA while conceding the remaining issues to the opponent’s repeatedly signaled valueD. At t=0.46 we are in mid-game and the opponent has shown no concession at all, so I am making a stronger but still self-protective concession rather than accepting their very low-utility all-D offer.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.55–0.60 while still holding firm on issueA, my highest-weight issue. The opponent has made no concessions at all, repeatedly offering all valueD, so I will not drop to their position yet; instead I concede substantially on lower-weight issues and avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.52 by holding firm on issueA, my highest-weight issue, while conceding fully on the lower-weight issues. At the midpoint there is some pressure to move toward the opponent, but their offers have remained fixed at all valueD with no concession, so I should not yet drop my most important issue. This also avoids repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.412 with this bid, which is a substantial concession but still clearly above reservation value. Since we are just past the midpoint and the opponent has repeatedly held firm at all valueD with no natural-language messages, I am moving closer to their apparent preference while avoiding the exact bid I made last turn.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.44, preserving some value on the most important issueA while conceding fully on issueB and issueC and mostly on issueD. At t=0.54 there is time pressure to move toward the opponent, but their offers have shown no concession at all, so I should not collapse all the way to their all-valueD bid yet. Since my previous bid was exactly issueA=valueB with all others valueD, I am varying the low-weight issueD slightly to avoid an exact repeat.

At t=0.56 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.41 while still preserving my most important issue better than the opponent’s latest offer. The opponent had held firm on all valueD bids until their latest move to issueA:valueC, so they are beginning to concede, but only slightly; I will reciprocate by offering them valueD on the lower-weight issues while asking for issueA:valueB rather than accepting issueA:valueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.44, slightly above my last offer while still keeping most issues at the opponent-favored valueD. The opponent held firm at all valueD for a long time and only recently moved issueA to valueC, so I will continue pressing for issueA=valueB while varying a low-weight issue to avoid repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.41 by holding some ground on the most important issueA while conceding fully on issueB, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.60 there is meaningful time pressure, and the opponent has finally moved from issueA:valueD to issueA:valueC, so I am countering with issueA:valueB as a realistic midpoint rather than dropping all the way to their latest offer. There are no opponent messages, and the offer history shows they are otherwise firm on valueD for the remaining issues.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.412 by holding issueA at valueB, since issueA is my most important issue, while conceding fully on issueB, issueC, and issueD. At t=0.62 there is some time pressure, but not enough to drop all the way to the opponent’s low-utility position; their offers show only a brief concession to issueA=valueC before reverting, so I will continue pressing for issueA=valueB.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.308 by offering issueA: valueC while conceding the other issues, which is above reservation but much closer to the opponent’s demonstrated position. At t=0.64 the negotiation is moving into the later phase, and the opponent has mostly held at all valueD but did previously offer this exact issueA concession, so this is the best acceptance-oriented bid supported by their history. There are no opponent messages to weigh.
